concerns.text: The plan proposes running Llama-3-8B in full precision on a 2-CPU GitHub Actions runner to generate 500 examples of DelTA coefficients. This is computationally infeasible (likely >24 hours) and risks OOM. The 'Fallback' strategy (logging timeout and proceeding with partial data) introduces severe selection bias: the subset of examples that complete first may differ systematically from those that timeout (e.g., shorter solutions, simpler math), invalidating the statistical representativeness of the ground truth.

The 'Semantic Similarity' feature uses cosine similarity against a 'seed set' of 50 GSM8K traces using 'Llama-3-8B last-layer embedding space'. This violates the 'Static-Input Independence' principle (Constitution Principle VI) and creates circularity. If the Oracle model (Llama-3-8B) generates the ground truth, and the Predictor uses embeddings from the *same* model architecture (even if frozen) to define features, the predictor is effectively using the Oracle's internal representation to predict the Oracle's output. This guarantees high correlation regardless of whether the signal is truly 'static' or 'emergent'.

The evaluation relies on Spearman rank correlation on a token-level dataset derived from only ~500 examples. With an average solution length of ~20-30 tokens, this yields ~10,000-15,000 data points, but these are highly clustered (non-i.i.d.) within examples. The plan does not account for intra-example correlation (tokens within the same problem are not independent). A permutation test shuffling individual tokens ignores this structure, likely inflating statistical significance (Type I error). The unit of analysis should be the example, not the token, or a cluster-robust variance estimator must be used.
